items.title: Menopause as a biological and psychological transition
items.author: Carolyn J. Gibson
items.content: There are complex interactions between physical changes during the menopause transition and mental health and wellbeing. In this Review, Thurston et al. integrate these interactions and outline behavioural approaches to managing menopausal symptoms and related mental health concerns.

items.title: Defining deliberation for dual-process models of reasoning
items.author: Wim De Neys
items.content: Dual-process models propose that reasoning involves intuition and deliberation, but the conceptualization of deliberation remains unclear. In this Perspective, De Neys presents four key functions of deliberation and proposes guiding principles for defining dual-process thinking.

items.title: Distinguishing performance gains from learning when using generative AI
items.author: Dragan Gašević
items.content: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly being integrated into education, where it can boost learners’ performance. However, these uses do not promote the deep cognitive and metacognitive processing that are required for high-quality learning.

items.title: Interdisciplinarity opens new frontiers for decision science
items.author: Sarah Vahed
items.content: Interdisciplinarity opens new frontiers for decision science

items.title: Facial expression production and perception in non-human primates
items.author: Jamie Whitehouse
items.content: Facial expressions are ubiquitous among primates and investigation of non-human primate facial behaviour is vital to an understanding of human facial behaviour. In this Review, Waller and colleagues discuss facial expression production and perception in non-human primates, focusing on methodological approaches and challenges.
